original: Aft Vámarð standa rúnar þar. En Varinn fáði þær, faðir, eft dauðan son.

translations.literal: After Vámóðr stand runes these. And Varinn colored them, father, after dead son.
translations.standard: In memory of Vámóðr stand these runes. And Varinn wrote them, the father, in memory of his dead son.

original: Segjum munnminni þat, hverjar tvær nágistir þær væri, þær er tólf sinnum varat nágifnar at helu, báðar saman miðlar ýmissum mönnum.

translations.literal: Say [as] folk-memory that, which two war-booties those were, which twelve times were taken as war-booty, both together shared [to] various men.
translations.standard: I say this as folk-memory, which the two war-booties were that twelve times were taken as war-booty, both together from various men.

original: Þat segjum annat, hverr fyrir níu öldum varð með Hreiðgotum lífi týna; ok dó hann með þeim fyrir sinni sök.

translations.literal: That say second, who before nine ages became with Hreiðgoths life lose; and died he with them for his guilt.
translations.standard: I say this second, who nine generations ago lost his life with the Hreiðgoths; and he died with them because of his guilt.

original: Réð Þjóðríkr hinn þormóði, stillir flutna, strandu Hreiðmarar. Sitr nú gærr á gota sínum, skjaldi of fatlaðr, skati Maringa.

translations.literal: Ruled Þjóðríkr the bold, lord [of] sea-warriors, shore [of] Hreið-sea. Sits now ready on horse his, shield over slung, prince [of] Maerings.
translations.standard: Theodoric the bold, lord of sea-warriors, ruled over the shores of the Hreið-sea. Now he sits ready on his Gothic horse, his shield slung over his shoulder, the prince of the Maerings.

original: Þat segjum tólfta, hvar hestr sé Gunnar etu vettvangi á, konungar tveir tigir sváir liggja.

translations.literal: That say twelfth, where horse may-be [of] Gunnr food-battlefield on, kings two tens [twenty] such lie.
translations.standard: I say this twelfth, where the horse of Gunnr [the wolf] sees food on the battlefield, where twenty kings lie dead.

original: Þat segjum þrettánda, hverir tveir tigir konungar sátu at Sjólundi fjóra vetr, nafni bbornir, fimm bræðr, Valkis synir, hveðnir átta, Haðula synir.

translations.literal: That say thirteenth, which two tens [twenty] kings sat at Sjólund four winters, [of] names born, five brothers, Valkir's sons, eight [of] Hveðnir, Haðulr's sons.
translations.standard: I say this thirteenth, which twenty kings sat in Sjólund for four years, born with [the same] names, five brothers, the sons of Valkir, and eight Hveðnirs, the sons of Haðulr.

original: Segjum munnminni þat, hverr of dæmi væri konunga sjá, hverr væri gjaldi goldinn eftir kvenna.

translations.literal: Say folk-memory that, who of example were kings this, who were payment paid after women.
translations.standard: I say this as folk-memory, which of these kings was paid a ransom for his wife.

original: Segjum þat munnminni, hverr væri bborinn af tveimr tigi konunga á vettvangi.

translations.literal: Say that folk-memory, who were born of two tens [twenty] kings on battlefield.
translations.standard: I say this as folk-memory, who was born of twenty kings on the battlefield.

original: Þat segjum tólfta, hvar hestr sé Gunnar etu vettvangi á, konungar tveir tigir sváir liggja.

translations.literal: That say twelfth, where horse may-be [of] Gunnr food-battlefield on, kings two tens [twenty] such lie.
translations.standard: I say this twelfth, where the horse of Gunnr [the wolf] sees food on the battlefield, where twenty kings lie dead.

original: Segjum munnminni, hvar ítrir menn sáu hring-vápn dreyra-drifin.

translations.literal: Say folk-memory, where noble men saw ring-weapons blood-drifted.
translations.standard: I say this as folk-memory, where noble men saw ring-weapons [swords] covered in blood.

original: Sátti tulu tía kulu aft sunu sinna. Trúa þann teljum við.

translations.literal: Set [he] counts ten balls after sons his. True that count we.
translations.standard: He set ten counters for his sons. We count that as true.

original: Segjum munnminni þat, hverr of dæmi væri konunga sjá. Segjum þat munnminni, hverr væri bborinn af tveimr tigi konunga á vettvangi.

translations.literal: Say folk-memory that, which of example were kings this. Say that folk-memory, who were born of two tens [twenty] kings on battlefield.
translations.standard: I say this as folk-memory, which of these kings was an example. I say this as folk-memory, who was born of twenty kings on the battlefield.

original: Þat segjum víg-tigi, hvar hestr sé Gunnar etu vettvangi á, konungar tveir tigir sváir liggja.

translations.literal: That say war-twenty [twenty-fold], where horse may-be [of] Gunnr food-battlefield on, kings two tens such lie.
translations.standard: I say this twenty-fold, where the horse of Gunnr sees food on the battlefield, where twenty kings lie dead.

original: Aft Vámarð standa rúnar þar. Varinn fáði eft dauðan son. Segjum munnminni þat.

translations.literal: After Vámóðr stand runes there. Varinn colored after dead son. Say folk-memory that.
translations.standard: In memory of Vámóðr stand these runes. Varinn wrote them in memory of his dead son. I say this as folk-memory.

original: Segjum munnminni, hvar ítrir menn sáu hring-vápn dreyra-drifin. Þat segjum víg-tigi.

translations.literal: Say folk-memory, where noble men saw ring-weapons blood-drifted. That say war-twenty.
translations.standard: I say this as folk-memory, where noble men saw ring-weapons covered in blood. I say this twenty-fold.
